focusAreas: Verify Hero.css .top-gradient is now scoped under .hero (check built CSS)
Verify Hero block gradient still renders correctly (not broken by scoping)
Verify no `hero` class leakage — Hero should not provide styles to other blocks
Both primary and secondary variations render correctly
No JS errors, ResizeObserver still works

testPageVerification.notes: 12 hero block instances found, all wrapped in takt/demo-container

rawContent.message: Content unchanged and no code modifications

html.message: HTML snapshot updated — content changed from previous snapshot but code was not modified. Change is expected: new snapshot captured after developer-fix applied CSS scoping. Full QA run performed.

build.notes: Build passes with zero errors. Pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in Accordion, Archive, ContentWithMedia, EventHero, FullWidthMedia, PostHero, Tabs — all pre-existing and unrelated to this change.

findings.status: correctly scoped — was previously bare .top-gradient, now scoped under .hero

cssScoping.notes: All three .top-gradient rules in built CSS are scoped to their respective block class. No bare/global .top-gradient selector exists anywhere in the built output.

gradientRendering.description: Hero block gradient renders correctly after scoping change
evidence: Page audit confirms .hero .top-gradient present in all 12 Hero block instances
Hero PHP template has 'hero relative overflow-visible' on the section wrapper, so .hero parent class is always present, making .hero .top-gradient resolve correctly
Screenshots at 375, 768, 1024, 1440px all show gradient rendering as expected — neon-green/yellow-green gradient visible in every Hero instance

classLeakage.description: Hero class namespace does not leak to PostHero or EventHero

pageAuditCheck.notes: No unscoped .top-gradient elements exist on the Hero test page

variationRendering.description: Both primary and secondary variations render correctly

findings.observedBehavior: Centered heading with max-w-[912px], larger heading font size (text-[2.5rem]/var(--text-header-0)), QuickNavigation inner block rendered where enabled, centered content layout
Left-aligned heading with max-w-[700px], buttons rendered below description, left-aligned content layout

findings.notes: Instances with and without images both render gradient correctly. Long heading (instance index 8) wraps cleanly without layout breakage.

jsResizeObserver.description: Hero.js ResizeObserver correctly uses scoped .hero .top-gradient selector
jsResizeObserver.evidence: Hero.js line 6 queries '.hero .top-gradient' — this matches the scoped selector and will only attach ResizeObserver to Hero block gradient elements, not PostHero or EventHero gradients. No JS errors observed in page load screenshots.
jsResizeObserver.resizeObserverLogic: Queries all .hero .top-gradient elements on DOMContentLoaded, finds nearest <main>, caps max-height to main.offsetHeight dynamically

sync.notes: taktician_validate_all sync check passed. Pre-existing warnings: missing @var docblock for blockVariation/enableQuickNav in Hero.php (minor, pre-existing), InspectorControls root mismatch (expected for Gutenberg editor blocks). No new sync issues introduced by this change.

registration.notes: block.json passes all schema, naming, file reference, child block, and attribute type checks

passedChecks: Hero.css .top-gradient is correctly scoped under .hero in source and built CSS (public/css/screen.css line 168)
PostHero.css scopes gradient under .post-hero (line 149), EventHero.css under .event-hero (line 193)
No bare/global .top-gradient selector exists in built output
Hero block gradient renders correctly at all 12 instances — scoping did not break gradient display
PostHero and EventHero wrappers no longer carry the hero class — class leakage eliminated
hero-inner renamed to post-hero-inner (PostHero) and event-hero-inner (EventHero)
Hero.js ResizeObserver uses .hero .top-gradient selector — correctly scoped, no PostHero/EventHero interference
Both primary (hero--primary) and secondary (hero--secondary) variations render correctly
Gradient visible at 375, 768, 1024, and 1440px across all browsers
Long heading (200+ chars) wraps cleanly without layout breakage
Instances with and without background images both render gradient correctly

visualObservations.375px: Mobile layout renders correctly. Gradient present and prominent (neon-green to off-white fade) in all instances. Headings wrap cleanly. Primary variation centered, secondary left-aligned. QuickNavigation renders inline below heading. No horizontal scroll observed.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet layout transitions cleanly. Gradient fills correctly. Image mask (bottom fade) works on instances with background images. Content padding increases appropriately. Both variations maintain correct alignment.
visualObservations.1024px: Near-desktop layout. Heading sizes step up correctly. Gradient height capped to main element height via ResizeObserver (expected behavior). Primary variant wider max-width heading visible.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop layout. All 12 instances display correctly. Primary variants show centered large headings with QuickNavigation. Secondary variants show left-aligned headings with buttons. Gradient is visible and correctly styled in all instances. No visual artifacts or broken styling observed.
